behaviors.description: The request sent to the external API always includes the complete list of valid taxonomy labels.
behaviors.justification: The module constructs a request payload with taxonomy labels. This is payload construction logic testable by mocking the API and asserting on call arguments. The failure (missing labels in payload) is caught without real network I/O.
behaviors.test_description: Mock the external API client. Call classify(document). Assert that the mocked API was called with a labels parameter containing the complete taxonomy list ['legal', 'technical', 'financial', 'other'].

behaviors.description: When the API returns a label that is not in the taxonomy, the module returns 'unknown' instead of forwarding the unexpected value to the caller.
behaviors.justification: This is deterministic label validation logic. Mock the API to return a label outside the taxonomy (e.g., 'weird_category'), then verify the module returns 'unknown' instead of forwarding it. No real API interaction needed.
behaviors.test_description: Mock the external API to return {'label': 'unexpected_domain', 'confidence': 0.95}. Call classify(document). Assert that the result is 'unknown', not 'unexpected_domain'.

behaviors.description: The classification result for the same document is the same across repeated calls.
behaviors.justification: The module should cache results to ensure consistency across repeated calls. Mock the API to return two different results ('legal' and 'technical') on successive calls. Call classify(same_doc) twice and verify both return the cached first result, proving the cache works. This tests deterministic caching logic without real API.
behaviors.test_description: Mock the external API to return {'label': 'legal'} on first call and {'label': 'technical'} on second call. Call classify(same_document) twice. Assert both results equal 'legal' (cache hit on second call), and the mocked API was called exactly once.

behaviors.description: The number of tokens consumed for a typical document stays within the configured per-call budget.
behaviors.justification: The real failure is 'the actual external API consumes more tokens than the budget for a typical document.' A mock with artificial token counts hides this failure—it cannot reveal whether the real API exceeds budget. To catch the real failure, the test must call the actual external API and measure real token usage. A Unit-level mock test would only catch over-budget error handling, not actual API behavior.
behaviors.test_description: Configure the module with a per-call token budget (e.g., 1000 tokens). Call classify(typical_document) against the real external API. Retrieve the actual token count from the API response. Assert that actual_tokens_used <= configured_budget.

behaviors.description: When the API signals that the request rate is too high, the module waits and retries rather than returning an error to the caller.
behaviors.justification: The retry logic (on HTTP 429, sleep and retry) is deterministic business logic encapsulated in the module. Mock the API to return HTTP 429 on the first call and HTTP 200 with a valid response on the second call. Verify the module returns the success result (not an error) and calls the API twice. The real failure (module returns error instead of retrying) is caught by this mock.
behaviors.test_description: Mock the external API to return HTTP 429 (rate limit) on first call and HTTP 200 with valid classification on second call. Call classify(document). Assert the result contains a valid classification (not an error), and the mocked API was called exactly 2 times, confirming retry occurred.
